_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
- **WINDOW CLOSING — Submit feedback on the Biosecurity Regulation 2016 sunset review before the deadline.** Queensland's Department of Primary Industries (DPI) sunset review of the Biosecurity Regulation 2016 closes at **5:00 pm AEST, Friday 29 May 2026** — today. The workspace must assess whether any proposed changes to prohibited/restricted matter lists, biosecurity zones (including fire ant, varroa mite, and banana biosecurity zones), livestock traceability requirements, or statewide entry restrictions affect routing, cargo acceptance, or phytosanitary documentation requirements on inbound air and road freight movements into Queensland. Failure to engage forfeits influence over the replacement Regulation. *Source: Sunset Review of Queensland's Biosecurity Regulation 2016, Department of Primary Industries, 2026. https://dpi.engagementhub.com.au/biosecurity-regulation-review*

- **ACTION REQUIRED — Audit all Queensland trust structures and fiduciary relationships against the Trusts Act 2025.** The Trusts Act 2025 (Qld) commenced on **28 April 2026**, repealing the Trusts Act 1973. The Act applies retrospectively to all existing trusts. The workspace must immediately verify whether any operational entities, cargo-holding trusts, client structures, or consignment arrangements in Queensland are governed by trust instruments that may require review against new codified trustee duties, expanded powers, and record-keeping obligations now in force. Trust deeds that conflict with the Act's default rules must be assessed for amendment. *Source: Trusts Act 2025, Queensland Parliamentary Counsel, assented May 2025, commenced 28 April 2026. https://www.legislation.qld.gov.au/view/whole/html/inforce/2026-05-02/act-2025-008*

- **MONITORING — Track the Regional Planning Interests (Condamine Alluvium) and Other Legislation Amendment Bill 2026 through Parliament.** The Bill was introduced 25 March 2026 and the Primary Industries and Resources Committee tabled its report on 8 May 2026. The Queensland Government has three months from that date (by ~8 August 2026) to respond. If enacted, the Bill will alter CSG/coal seam gas approvals, remove the requirement for Regional Interest Development Approvals (RIDAs) in the Condamine Alluvium CSG area, and expand landholder compensation for CSG-induced subsidence. Workspaces handling agricultural or resources-sector freight through the Western Downs, Toowoomba, or Southern Downs regions should monitor for commencement to assess cargo routing or access-land implications. *Source: Regional Planning Interests (Condamine Alluvium) and Other Legislation Amendment Bill 2026, Primary Industries and Resources Committee Report No. 19, tabled 8 May 2026. https://www.parliament.qld.gov.au/Work-of-Committees/Committees/Committee-Details?cid=0&id=8574*

- **MONITORING — Note Environmental and Other Legislation Amendment Regulation 2026 in force as of 22 May 2026.** This Regulation amends the Environmental Protection Regulation 2019, Nature Conservation (Animals) Regulation 2020, Nature Conservation (Plants) Regulation 2020, and Waste Reduction and Recycling Regulation 2023. Key changes include ERA standards for petroleum exploration updated to version 3, and reclassification of conservation status for one fauna species and ten flora species. Workspaces moving fauna, flora, or environmentally sensitive cargo through Queensland should confirm that species and permit documentation aligns with the updated classification lists. *Source: Environmental Policy and Legislation Changes, Department of the Environment, Tourism, Science and Innovation, Queensland, 22 May 2026. https://environment.qld.gov.au/management/policy-regulation/changes*
